1 # Agents Observe 2 3 Real-time observability dashboard for Claude Code agents. 4 5 Includes powerful filtering, searching, and visualization of multi-agent sessions. 6 7

8 9 10 11

12 13

14 Demo video 15

16 17

18 19

20 21 The server and dashboard run locally or remotely, allowing multiple Claude Code instances to log full session data using hooks. 22 23 Hooks are used instead of OTEL to capture the full picture of agent actions. 24 25 ## Plugin Installation 26 27 ### Prerequisites 28 29 - [Docker](https://www.docker.com/) (required — the server runs as a container) 30 - [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) (required — hook scripts run via `node`) 31 32 ### Install 33 34 1. Add the marketplace: 35 36 ```bash 37 claude plugin marketplace add simple10/agents-observe 38 ``` 39 40 2. Install the plugin: 41 42 ```bash 43 claude plugin install agents-observe 44 ``` 45 46 3. Restart Claude Code. 47 48 That's it. On your next session, the server auto-starts as a Docker container and hooks begin capturing events. Open **** to see the dashboard. 49 50 ## Plugin Skills 51 52 | Skill | Description | 53 |-------|-------------| 54 | `/observe` | Open the dashboard URL and check if the server is running | 55 | `/observe status` | Check server health and show dashboard URL | 56 57 ## Why observability matters 58 59 When Claude Code runs autonomously — spawning subagents, calling tools, reading files, executing commands — you have no visibility into what's actually happening. The terminal shows a fraction of the activity. Subagents are invisible. Tool calls blur together. And when something goes wrong three agents deep in a parallel execution, you're left reading through logs after the fact. 60 61 Claude Observe captures every hook event as it happens and streams it to a live dashboard. You see exactly what each agent is doing, which tools it's calling, what files it's touching, and how subagents relate to their parents. In real time. 62 63 This matters because: 64 65 - **Multi-agent work is opaque.** A coordinator spawns a code reviewer, a test runner, and a documentation agent in parallel. Without observability, you only see the final result. With it, you watch each agent work and catch problems as they happen. 66 - **Tool calls are the ground truth.** The assistant's text output is a summary. The actual tool calls — the Bash commands, file reads, edits, grep patterns — tell you what Claude is really doing. Claude Observe shows you both. 67 - **Debugging is time travel.** When a subagent makes a bad edit or runs a destructive command, you need to trace back through the exact sequence of events. The event stream gives you that timeline with full payloads. 68 - **Sessions are ephemeral, but patterns aren't.** By capturing events across sessions, you can see how agents behave over time, which tools they favor, and where they get stuck. 69 70 ## What you can do 71 72 - Watch tool calls stream in as they happen (PreToolUse → PostToolUse with results) 73 - See the full agent hierarchy — which subagent was spawned by which parent 74 - Filter by agent, tool type, or search across all events 75 - Expand any event to see the full payload, command, and result 76 - Click timeline icons to jump to specific events in the stream 77 - Browse historical sessions with human-readable names (e.g., "twinkly-hugging-dragon") 78 79 ## Architecture 80 81 ``` 82 Claude Code Hooks → observe_cli.mjs → API Server (SQLite) → React Dashboard 83 (dumb pipe) (HTTP POST) (parse + store) (WebSocket live) 84 ``` 85 86 The hook script is a dumb pipe — it reads the raw event from stdin, adds the project name, and POSTs it to the server. The server parses events, stores agent metadata (name, type, parentage), and forwards events to subscribed WebSocket clients. The React dashboard derives all agent state (status, event counts, timing) from the event stream — the server is a dumb store. 87 88 ## Standalone Installation 89 90 > For development or running without the plugin. If you installed via the plugin above, skip this section. 91 92 ### 1. Clone and install dependencies 93 94 ```bash 95 git clone https://github.com/simple10/agents-observe.git agents-observe 96 cd agents-observe 97 98 # Install just if needed 99 brew install just 100 101 # Start the docker container 102 just start 103 104 # Or start local dev servers 105 just install 106 just dev 107 ``` 108 109 See [justfile](./justfile) for additional commands. 110 111 ### 2. Configure Claude Code hooks 112 113 Generate the hooks config for your project: 114 115 ```bash 116 just setup-hooks my-project 117 ``` 118 119 This prints a JSON snippet with all paths pre-filled. Copy it into your Claude Code settings at either: 120 121 - **Project-level** (recommended): `.claude/settings.json` in your project root 122 - **User-level** (all projects): `~/.claude/settings.json` 123 124 **Environment variables set in the config:** 125 126 | Variable | Default | Description | 127 |----------|---------|-------------| 128 | `AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG` | (auto-detected) | Project slug shown in the dashboard URL. If not set, derived from the session transcript path. | 129 | `AGENTS_OBSERVE_API_BASE_URL` | `http://127.0.0.1:4981/api` | Full URL for the events endpoint | 130 131 ### 3. Verify it works 132 133 ```bash 134 # Check the server is running 135 just health 136 137 # Send a test event 138 just test-event 139 ``` 140 141 Navigate to **** (dev) or **** (Docker). You should see the test event appear. Start a Claude Code session in your configured project and events will stream in automatically. 142 143 ## Standalone Commands 144 145 If you have [just](https://github.com/casey/just) installed: 146 147 ```bash 148 # Local Dev Commands: 149 just install # Install all dependencies 150 just dev # Start server + client in dev mode (hot reload) 151 just dev-server # Start only the server 152 just dev-client # Start only the client 153 just dev-client-build # Build the client for production 154 just test # Run all tests (server + client) 155 just test-event # Send a test event to the server 156 just fmt # Format all source files 157 158 # Docker Container Commands: 159 just build # Build the Docker image locally 160 just start # Start production containers (Docker, detached) 161 just stop # Stop Docker containers 162 just restart # Restart Docker containers 163 just logs # Follow Docker container logs 164 165 # Local Server Commands: 166 just start-local # Builds client and runs local server (without docker) 167 npm run start # Same as `just start-local` 168 169 # Setup & Utilities: 170 just setup-hooks # Generate hooks config for a project 171 just health # Check server health 172 just cli # Run the CLI (hook, health, start, stop, restart) 173 just db-reset # Delete the events database 174 just open # Open the dashboard in browser 175 ``` 176 177 ## Project structure 178 179 ```text 180 app/ 181 server/ # Node server — Hono routes, SQLite, WebSocket 182 client/ # React 19 + shadcn dashboard 183 hooks/ 184 hooks.json # Plugin hook definitions 185 scripts/ # CLI, MCP server, and shared libs 186 skills/ # /observe and /observe status skills 187 scripts/ # Release tooling 188 test/ # Integration tests 189 data/ # SQLite database (auto-created) 190 docs/ # Screenshots and demo assets 191 .claude-plugin/ # Plugin + marketplace manifests 192 .env # Env config options used by cli & local server 193 .mcp.json # MCP server configuration 194 Dockerfile # Production container image 195 docker-compose.yml # Container orchestration 196 justfile # Task runner commands 197 start.mjs # Docker container entrypoint 198 settings.template.json # Hooks config template for setup-hooks 199 vitest.config.ts # Test configuration 200 package.json # Version metadata and workspace scripts 201 ``` 202 203 ## How it works 204 205 **Hooks** fire on every Claude Code event (tool calls, prompts, stops, subagent lifecycle). The hook script reads the raw event from stdin, adds the project name, and POSTs it to the server. If the server needs additional data (like the session's human-readable slug), it responds with a request — the hook reads it from the local transcript file and sends it back. 206 207 **Server** receives raw events, extracts structural fields (type, tool name, agent ID), stores agent metadata (name, description, type, parentage), and saves everything in SQLite. Events are forwarded to WebSocket clients subscribed to the relevant session — each browser tab only receives events for the session it's viewing. The server tracks session status (active/stopped) but does not track agent status. 208 209 **Client** fetches events via REST API on initial load, then receives real-time updates via WebSocket (events are appended to the local cache — no refetching). All agent state (status, event counts, timing) is derived from the event stream. Tool events are deduped client-side (PreToolUse + PostToolUse merged into a single row). The emoji icon mapping and summary generation are editable config files. 210 211 ### Dev vs Production 212 213 In dev mode, the client and server run as separate processes with separate ports. 214 215 In production or docker mode, the client is bundled and served by the server. Both the API and dashboard are served from the same process and port. 216 217 Both local dev and Docker flows default to using the same sqlite database in ./data. The database is auto created as needed. 218 219 ## Troubleshooting 220 221 **Docker not running?** 222 223 The plugin requires Docker to run the server. Make sure Docker Desktop (or the Docker daemon) is running, then restart Claude Code. 224 225 **Port 4981 in use?** 226 227 If another process is using port 4981, stop it or remove a stale container: 228 229 ```bash 230 docker stop agents-observe && docker rm agents-observe 231 ``` 232 233 **Plugin not capturing events?** 234 235 Run `/observe status` to check if the server is running. If the container doesn't exist, restart Claude Code. Check Docker logs with `docker logs agents-observe`. 236 237 **Events not appearing in the dashboard?** 238 239 1. **Is the server running?** Run `just health` to check. 240 2. **Is the hook script configured?** Run `just setup-hooks my-project` and verify the output matches your `.claude/settings.json`. 241 3. **Is `AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG` set?** If `AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG` is not set, the project is auto-detected from the session transcript path. 242 4. **Can the hook reach the server?** Run `just test-event` — if the event appears in the dashboard, the server is reachable. 243 244 **WebSocket disconnected?** 245 246 The client reconnects automatically every 3 seconds if the WebSocket connection drops. You'll see "Disconnected" in the sidebar footer. Events received during reconnection will appear once the connection is restored and the events are refetched. 247 248 **Database issues?** 249 250 Run `just db-reset` to delete the SQLite database and start fresh. The database is auto-created on the next server start. 251 252 ## ROADMAP 253 254 - [ ] Add support for Codex 255 - [ ] Add support for OpenClaw 256 - [ ] Add support for pi-code agents 257 258 -- 259 260 ## Reference 261 262 - [Claude Hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md) - official list of currently supported hooks 263 264 ## Related Projects 265 266 - [Agent Super Spy](https://github.com/simple10/agent-super-spy) - full observability stack for agents, can be run locally or remotely 267 - [Multi-Agent Observability System](https://github.com/disler/claude-code-hooks-multi-agent-observability) - inspired this project 268 - [Claude DevTools](https://github.com/matt1398/claude-devtools) - visualization for claude session files, requires running on local machine 269 270 ## License 271 272 MIT 273

283	  async getEventsForSession(sessionId: string, filters?: EventFilters): Promise<StoredEvent[]> {
284	    let sql = 'SELECT * FROM events WHERE session_id = ?'
285	    const params: any[] = [sessionId]
286	
287	    if (filters?.agentIds && filters.agentIds.length > 0) {
288	      const placeholders = filters.agentIds.map(() => '?').join(',')
289	      sql += ` AND agent_id IN (${placeholders})`
290	      params.push(...filters.agentIds)
291	    }
292	
293	    if (filters?.type) {
294	      sql += ' AND type = ?'
295	      params.push(filters.type)
296	    }
297	
298	    if (filters?.subtype) {
299	      sql += ' AND subtype = ?'
300	      params.push(filters.subtype)
301	    }
302	
303	    if (filters?.search) {
304	      sql += ' AND (summary LIKE ? OR payload LIKE ?)'
305	      const term = `%${filters.search}%`
306	      params.push(term, term)
307	    }
308	
309	    sql += ' ORDER BY timestamp ASC'
310	
311	    if (filters?.limit) {
312	      sql += ' LIMIT ?'
313	      params.push(filters.limit)
314	      if (filters?.offset) {
315	        sql += ' OFFSET ?'
316	        params.push(filters.offset)

333	  async getThreadForEvent(eventId: number): Promise<StoredEvent[]> {
334	    const event = this.db.prepare('SELECT * FROM events WHERE id = ?').get(eventId) as
335	      | StoredEvent
336	      | undefined
337	    if (!event) return []
338	
339	    const sessionId = event.session_id
340	    const agentId = event.agent_id
341	
342	    // For SubagentStop or events from a non-root agent:
343	    // return all events belonging to that specific agent
344	    const isSubagent = agentId !== sessionId
345	    if (event.subtype === 'SubagentStop' || isSubagent) {
346	      return this.db
347	        .prepare('SELECT * FROM events WHERE agent_id = ? ORDER BY timestamp ASC')
348	        .all(agentId) as StoredEvent[]
349	    }
350	
351	    // For root agent events: find the turn boundary (Prompt -> Stop)
352	    const prevPrompt = this.db
353	      .prepare(
354	        `SELECT timestamp FROM events
355	         WHERE session_id = ? AND subtype = 'UserPromptSubmit' AND timestamp <= ?
356	         ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1`,
357	      )
358	      .get(sessionId, event.timestamp) as { timestamp: number } | undefined
359	
360	    const startTs = prevPrompt ? prevPrompt.timestamp : 0
361	
362	    // End at the first Stop or next UserPromptSubmit
363	    const nextBoundary = this.db
364	      .prepare(
365	        `SELECT timestamp FROM events
366	         WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp > ?
367	           AND (subtype = 'UserPromptSubmit' OR subtype = 'Stop' OR subtype = 'SubagentStop')
368	         ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT 1`,
369	      )
370	      .get(sessionId, startTs) as { timestamp: number } | undefined
371	
372	    const endTs = nextBoundary ? nextBoundary.timestamp : Infinity
373	
374	    if (endTs === Infinity) {
375	      return this.db
376	        .prepare(
377	          'SELECT * FROM events WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ? ORDER BY timestamp ASC',
378	        )
379	        .all(sessionId, startTs) as StoredEvent[]
380	    }
381	
382	    return this.db
383	      .prepare(
384	        'SELECT * FROM events WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ? AND timestamp <= ? ORDER BY timestamp ASC',
385	      )
386	      .all(sessionId, startTs, endTs) as StoredEvent[]
387	  }

442	      LEFT JOIN agents a ON a.session_id = s.id
443	      LEFT JOIN events e ON e.session_id = s.id
444	      GROUP BY s.id

452	  async healthCheck(): Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }> {
453	    try {
454	      const row = this.db.prepare('SELECT 1 AS ok').get() as { ok: number } | undefined
455	      if (row?.ok !== 1) return { ok: false, error: 'SQLite query returned unexpected result' }
456	
457	      // Verify tables exist
458	      const tables = this.db
459	        .prepare("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('projects','sessions','events','agents')")
460	        .all() as { name: string }[]
461	      if (tables.length < 4) {
462	        const missing = ['projects', 'sessions', 'events', 'agents'].filter(
463	          (t) => !tables.some((r) => r.name === t),
464	        )
465	        return { ok: false, error: `Missing tables: ${missing.join(', ')}` }
466	      }
467	
468	      return { ok: true }
469	    } catch (err: any) {
470	      return { ok: false, error: err.message || 'Unknown database error' }

1	#!/usr/bin/env node
2	// hooks/scripts/observe_cli.mjs
3	// CLI entrypoint for Agents Observe plugin.
4	// Commands: hook, health, restart
5	
6	import { getConfig } from './lib/config.mjs'
7	import { getJson, postJson } from './lib/http.mjs'
8	import { createLogger } from './lib/logger.mjs'
9	import { handleCallbackRequests } from './lib/callbacks.mjs'
10	import { startServer, stopServer } from './lib/docker.mjs'
11	
12	const cliArgs = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2))
13	const config = getConfig(cliArgs)
14	const log = createLogger('cli.log', config)
15	
16	switch (cliArgs.commands[0] || 'help') {
17	  case 'help':
18	    console.log('Usage: node observe_cli.mjs <command> [--base-url URL] [--project-slug SLUG]')
19	    console.log('Commands: hook, health, start, stop,restart')
20	    console.log('  hook: Send an event to the server')
21	    console.log('  health: Check the server health')
22	    console.log('  start: Restart the server')
23	    console.log('  stop: Stop the server')
24	    console.log('  restart: Restart the server')
25	    process.exit(0)
26	  case 'hook':
27	    hookCommand()
28	    break
29	  case 'health':
30	    healthCommand()
31	    break
32	  case 'start':
33	    startCommand()
34	    break
35	  case 'stop':
36	    stopCommand()
37	    break
38	  case 'restart':
39	    startCommand('Restarting server...')
40	    break
41	  default:
42	    console.error(`Unknown command: ${cliArgs.commands[0]}`)
43	    console.error(
44	      'Usage: node observe_cli.mjs <hook|health|restart> [--base-url URL] [--project-slug SLUG]',
45	    )
46	    process.exit(1)
47	}
48	
49	// -- Commands -----------------------------------------------------
50	
51	function hookCommand() {
52	  log.trace('CLI hook command invoked')
53	
54	  let input = ''
55	  process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')
56	  process.stdin.on('data', (chunk) => {
57	    input += chunk
58	  })
59	  process.stdin.on('end', () => {
60	    if (!input.trim()) {
61	      log.trace('Empty stdin, skipping')
62	      return
63	    }
64	
65	    let hookPayload
66	    try {
67	      hookPayload = JSON.parse(input)
68	    } catch (err) {
69	      log.warn(`Failed to parse hook payload: ${err.message}`)
70	      return
71	    }
72	
73	    const hookEvent = hookPayload.event || 'unknown'
74	    const toolName = hookPayload.tool_name || hookPayload.tool?.name || ''
75	    log.debug(`Hook event: ${hookEvent}${toolName ? ` tool=${toolName}` : ''}`)
76	    log.trace(`Hook payload: ${input.trim().slice(0, 500)}`)
77	
78	    const envelope = { hook_payload: hookPayload, meta: { env: {} } }
79	    if (config.projectSlug) {
80	      envelope.meta.env.AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG = config.projectSlug
81	    }
82	
83	    // Send hook payload to API server
84	    postJson(`${config.apiBaseUrl}/events`, envelope, {
85	      fireAndForget: config.allowedCallbacks.size === 0,
86	      log,
87	    })
88	      .then((result) => {
89	        if (result.status === 0) {
90	          log.error(`Server unreachable at ${config.baseOrigin}: ${result.error}`)
91	          return
92	        }
93	        log.trace(`Server response: status=${result.status} hasRequests=${!!result.body?.requests}`)
94	        if (result.body?.requests) {
95	          // Handle callback requests from the server
96	          // Used to patch sessions info
97	          return handleCallbackRequests(result.body.requests, { config, log })
98	        }
99	      })
100	      .catch((err) => {
101	        log.error(`Hook POST failed: ${err.message}`)
102	      })
103	  })
104	}
105	
106	/**
107	 * Get health and runtime info about the server
108	 *
109	 * Used by observe-status skill
110	 */
111	async function healthCommand(exit = true) {
112	  log.trace('CLI health command invoked')
113	  const healthUrl = `${config.apiBaseUrl}/health`
114	  const result = await getJson(healthUrl, { log })
115	  if (result.status === 200 && result.body?.ok) {
116	    const b = result.body
117	    const isDocker = b.runtime === 'docker'
118	    const runtime = isDocker ? `Docker` : 'local server'
119	
120	    console.log(`Raw ${healthUrl} response:`)
121	    console.log(JSON.stringify(b, null, 2))
122	    console.log('')
123	    console.log('Hooks CLI (local):')
124	    console.log(`  CLI Path: ${config.cliPath}`)
125	    console.log(`  Log Level: ${config.logLevel || 'unknown'}`)
126	    console.log(`  Logs: ${config.logsDir}`)
127	    console.log(
128	      `  Allowed Callbacks: ${
129	        config.allowedCallbacks.size ? [...config.allowedCallbacks].join(', ') : 'none'
130	      }`,
131	    )
132	    console.log('')
133	    console.log(`Agents Observe Server (${runtime}):`)
134	    console.log(`  Version: v${b.version || 'unknown'}`)
135	    console.log(`  Dashboard: ${config.baseOrigin}`)
136	    console.log(`  API: ${config.apiBaseUrl}`)
137	    console.log(`  Runtime: ${runtime}`)
138	    if (isDocker) {
139	      console.log(`  Container Name: ${config.containerName}`)
140	      console.log(`  Image: ${config.dockerImage}`)
141	      console.log(`  Data Dir: ${config.dataDir} (bind mounted)`)
142	    } else {
143	      console.log(`  Database: ${b.dbPath || 'unknown'}`)
144	    }
145	    console.log(`  Log Level: ${b.logLevel || 'unknown'}`)
146	
147	    // Version mismatch detection
148	    if (config.expectedVersion && b.version && config.expectedVersion !== b.version) {
149	      console.log('')
150	      console.log(`⚠ Version mismatch: CLI is v${config.expectedVersion}, server is v${b.version}`)
151	      console.log(`  To update the server, run: node ${config.cliPath} restart`)
152	    }
153	    exit && process.exit(0)
154	  } else if (result.status === 0) {
155	    console.log(`Agents Observe server is not running.`)
156	    console.log(`  Checked: ${healthUrl}`)
157	    console.log(`  Error: ${result.error || 'connection refused'}`)
158	    exit && process.exit(1)
159	  } else {
160	    console.log(`Agents Observe server error (HTTP ${result.status}):`)
161	    console.log(JSON.stringify(result.body, null, 2))
162	    exit && process.exit(1)
163	  }
164	}
165	
166	/**
167	 * Restart the Docker container (pulls latest image for current CLI version).
168	 */
169	async function startCommand(msg = 'Starting server...') {
170	  log.info(msg)
171	  const actualPort = await startServer(config, log)
172	  if (actualPort) {
173	    await healthCommand(false)
174	    console.log(`\nServer started on port ${actualPort}`)
175	    console.log(`  Dashboard: http://127.0.0.1:${actualPort}`)
176	  } else {
177	    console.error('Failed to start server')
178	    process.exit(1)
179	  }
180	}
181	
182	/**
183	 * Stop the Docker container (pulls latest image for current CLI version).
184	 */
185	async function stopCommand() {
186	  await stopServer(config, log)
187	  log.info('Server stopped')
188	}
189	// -- Helpers ------------------------------------------------------
190	
191	function parseArgs(args) {
192	  const parsed = { commands: [], baseUrl: null, projectSlug: null }
193	  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
194	    if (args[i] === '--base-url' && args[i + 1]) {
195	      parsed.baseUrl = args[i + 1]
196	      i++
197	    } else if (args[i] === '--project-slug' && args[i + 1]) {
198	      parsed.projectSlug = args[i + 1]
199	      i++
200	    } else if (!args[i].startsWith('-')) {
201	      parsed.commands.push(args[i])
202	    }
203	  }
204	  return parsed
205	}
206

1	// app/server/src/parser.ts
2	// Extracts structural fields from raw JSONL events.
3	// NO formatting, NO truncation, NO summary generation — that's the client's job.
4	
5	export interface ParsedRawEvent {
6	  projectName: string | null
7	  sessionId: string
8	  slug: string | null
9	  transcriptPath: string | null
10	  type: string
11	  subtype: string | null
12	  toolName: string | null
13	  toolUseId: string | null
14	  timestamp: number
15	  // The agent this event belongs to (from payload.agent_id — present on subagent hook events)
16	  ownerAgentId: string | null
17	  // The subagent being spawned/stopped (from Agent tool response or SubagentStop)
18	  subAgentId: string | null
19	  subAgentName: string | null
20	  subAgentDescription: string | null
21	  metadata: Record<string, unknown>
22	  raw: Record<string, unknown>
23	}
24	
25	export function parseRawEvent(raw: Record<string, unknown>): ParsedRawEvent {
26	  const projectName = (raw.project_name as string) || null
27	  const sessionId = (raw.session_id as string) || (raw.sessionId as string) || 'unknown'
28	  const slug = (raw.slug as string) || null
29	  const transcriptPath = (raw.transcript_path as string) || null
30	  const timestamp = parseTimestamp(raw.timestamp)
31	  const toolUseId = (raw.tool_use_id as string) || null
32	  // agent_id is present on hook events fired from subagents
33	  const ownerAgentId = (raw.agent_id as string) || null
34	
35	  let type: string
36	  let subtype: string | null = null
37	  let toolName: string | null = null
38	  let subAgentId: string | null = null
39	  let subAgentName: string | null = null
40	  let subAgentDescription: string | null = null
41	
42	  const hookEventName = raw.hook_event_name as string | undefined
43	
44	  if (hookEventName) {
45	    // === HOOK FORMAT ===
46	    const hookToolName = raw.tool_name as string | undefined
47	    const toolInput = raw.tool_input as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
48	
49	    switch (hookEventName) {
50	      case 'SessionStart':
51	        type = 'session'
52	        subtype = 'SessionStart'
53	        break
54	      case 'UserPromptSubmit':
55	        type = 'user'
56	        subtype = 'UserPromptSubmit'
57	        break
58	      case 'PreToolUse':
59	        type = 'tool'
60	        subtype = 'PreToolUse'
61	        toolName = hookToolName || null
62	        if (toolName === 'Agent') {
63	          subAgentName = (toolInput?.name as string) || null
64	          subAgentDescription = (toolInput?.description as string) || null
65	        }
66	        break
67	      case 'PostToolUse':
68	        type = 'tool'
69	        subtype = 'PostToolUse'
70	        toolName = hookToolName || null
71	        // Extract subagent info from Agent tool response
72	        if (toolName === 'Agent') {
73	          const toolResponse = raw.tool_response as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
74	          if (toolResponse) {
75	            subAgentId = (toolResponse.agentId as string) || null
76	            subAgentName = (toolInput?.name as string) || null
77	            subAgentDescription = (toolInput?.description as string) || null
78	          }
79	        }
80	        break
81	      case 'Stop':
82	        type = 'system'
83	        subtype = 'Stop'
84	        break
85	      case 'SubagentStop':
86	        type = 'system'
87	        subtype = 'SubagentStop'
88	        subAgentId = (raw.agent_id as string) || null
89	        break
90	      case 'PostToolUseFailure':
91	        type = 'tool'
92	        subtype = 'PostToolUseFailure'
93	        toolName = hookToolName || null
94	        break
95	      case 'Notification':
96	        type = 'system'
97	        subtype = 'Notification'
98	        break
99	      default:
100	        type = 'system'
101	        subtype = hookEventName
102	        break
103	    }
104	  } else {
105	    // === TRANSCRIPT JSONL FORMAT ===
106	    type = (raw.type as string) || 'unknown'
107	
108	    if (raw.subtype) {
109	      subtype = raw.subtype as string
110	    }
111	
112	    const data = raw.data as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
113	    const message = raw.message as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
114	    const toolUseResult = raw.toolUseResult as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
115	
116	    if (type === 'progress' && data) {
117	      const dataType = data.type as string
118	
119	      if (dataType === 'hook_progress') {
120	        subtype = (data.hookEvent as string) || null
121	        const hookName = data.hookName as string
122	        if (hookName && hookName.includes(':')) {
123	          toolName = hookName.split(':').slice(1).join(':')
124	        }
125	      }
126	
127	      if (dataType === 'agent_progress') {
128	        subtype = 'agent_progress'
129	        subAgentId = (data.agentId as string) || null
130	        const nestedMsg = data.message as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
131	        if (nestedMsg?.message) {
132	          const innerMsg = nestedMsg.message as Record<string, unknown>
133	          const content = innerMsg.content
134	          if (Array.isArray(content)) {
135	            const toolUse = content.find((c: any) => c.type === 'tool_use') as
136	              | Record<string, unknown>
137	              | undefined
138	            if (toolUse) {
139	              toolName = (toolUse.name as string) || null
140	            }
141	          }
142	        }
143	      }
144	    }
145	
146	    if (type === 'assistant' && message) {
147	      const content = message.content
148	      if (Array.isArray(content)) {
149	        const toolUse = content.find((c: any) => c.type === 'tool_use') as
150	          | Record<string, unknown>
151	          | undefined
152	        if (toolUse) {
153	          toolName = (toolUse.name as string) || null
154	          if (toolName === 'Agent') {
155	            const input = toolUse.input as Record<string, unknown> | undefined
156	            subAgentName = (input?.name as string) || null
157	            subAgentDescription = (input?.description as string) || null
158	          }
159	        }
160	      }
161	    }
162	
163	    if (toolUseResult) {
164	      subAgentId = (toolUseResult.agentId as string) || subAgentId
165	    }
166	  }
167	
168	  const metadata: Record<string, unknown> = {}
169	  for (const key of [
170	    'version',
171	    'gitBranch',
172	    'cwd',
173	    'entrypoint',
174	    'permissionMode',
175	    'userType',
176	    'permission_mode',
177	  ]) {
178	    if (raw[key] !== undefined) metadata[key] = raw[key]
179	  }
180	
181	  return {
182	    projectName,
183	    sessionId,
184	    slug,
185	    transcriptPath,
186	    type,
187	    subtype,
188	    toolName,
189	    toolUseId,
190	    timestamp,
191	    ownerAgentId,
192	    subAgentId,
193	    subAgentName,
194	    subAgentDescription,
195	    metadata,
196	    raw,
197	  }
198	}
199	
200	function parseTimestamp(ts: unknown): number {
201	  if (typeof ts === 'number') return ts
202	  if (typeof ts === 'string') {
203	    const parsed = new Date(ts).getTime()
204	    return isNaN(parsed) ? Date.now() : parsed
205	  }
206	  return Date.now()
207	}
208

1	// app/server/src/app.ts
2	import { Hono } from 'hono'
3	import { cors } from 'hono/cors'
4	import { serveStatic } from '@hono/node-server/serve-static'
5	import path from 'path'
6	import fs from 'fs'
7	import type { EventStore } from './storage/types'
8	import { config } from './config'
9	
10	import eventsRouter from './routes/events'
11	import projectsRouter from './routes/projects'

18	type Env = {
19	  Variables: {
20	    store: EventStore

22	    broadcastToAll: (msg: object) => void
23	  }

26	export function createApp(
27	  store: EventStore,
28	  broadcastToSession: (sessionId: string, msg: object) => void,
29	  broadcastToAll: (msg: object) => void,
30	) {
31	  const app = new Hono<Env>()
32	
33	  app.use('*', cors())
34	
35	  // Inject store and broadcast into all routes
36	  app.use('*', async (c, next) => {
37	    c.set('store', store)
38	    c.set('broadcastToSession', broadcastToSession)
39	    c.set('broadcastToAll', broadcastToAll)
40	    await next()

46	  app.route('/api', agentsRouter)
47	  app.route('/api', adminRouter)
48	  app.route('/api', healthRouter)

50	
51	  // Serve built client static files when clientDistPath is configured
52	  const clientDistPath = config.clientDistPath
53	  if (clientDistPath && fs.existsSync(clientDistPath)) {
54	    app.use('/*', serveStatic({ root: path.relative(process.cwd(), clientDistPath) }))
55	
56	    // Return 404 for unmatched API routes before SPA fallback
57	    app.all('/api/*', (c) => c.json({ error: 'Not found' }, 404))
58

61	    app.get('*', (c) => c.html(indexHtml))
62	  }
63	
64	  return app

1	// app/server/src/routes/events.ts
2	import { Hono } from 'hono'
3	import type { EventStore } from '../storage/types'
4	import type { ParsedEvent } from '../types'
5	import { parseRawEvent } from '../parser'
6	import { resolveProject } from '../services/project-resolver'
7	import { config } from '../config'
8	
9	type Env = {
10	  Variables: {
11	    store: EventStore
12	    broadcastToSession: (sessionId: string, msg: object) => void
13	    broadcastToAll: (msg: object) => void
14	  }
15	}
16	
17	const router = new Hono<Env>()
18	
19	const LOG_LEVEL = config.logLevel
20	
21	// Track root agent IDs per session (sessionId -> agentId)
22	const sessionRootAgents = new Map<string, string>()
23	
24	// Track pending Agent tool metadata so we can name subagents early.
25	// When PreToolUse:Agent fires, we store name+description keyed by tool_use_id
26	// and also push onto a per-session FIFO queue. The queue is necessary because
27	// subagent events carry only agent_id (not the parent tool_use_id), so we can't
28	// directly look up by tool_use_id when a new subagent first appears.
29	//
30	// When multiple Agent tools are invoked concurrently (e.g. two subagents spawned
31	// in the same turn), each gets its own queue entry so names are assigned 1:1.
32	interface PendingAgentMeta { name: string | null; description: string | null }
33	const pendingAgentMeta = new Map<string, PendingAgentMeta>() // toolUseId -> { name, description }
34	const pendingAgentTypes = new Map<string, string>() // toolUseId -> subagent_type
35	const pendingAgentMetaQueue = new Map<string, PendingAgentMeta[]>() // sessionId -> FIFO queue
36	const namedAgents = new Map<string, Set<string>>() // sessionId -> set of agent IDs already named via queue
37	
38	async function ensureRootAgent(
39	  store: EventStore,
40	  sessionId: string,
41	): Promise<string> {
42	  let rootId = sessionRootAgents.get(sessionId)
43	  if (!rootId) {
44	    rootId = sessionId
45	    await store.upsertAgent(rootId, sessionId, null, null, null)
46	    sessionRootAgents.set(sessionId, rootId)
47	  }
48	  return rootId
49	}
50	
51	// POST /events
52	router.post('/events', async (c) => {
53	  const store = c.get('store')
54	  const broadcastToSession = c.get('broadcastToSession')
55	  const broadcastToAll = c.get('broadcastToAll')
56	
57	  try {
58	    const body = await c.req.json()
59	
60	    // Support both envelope format and legacy flat format
61	    let hookPayload: Record<string, unknown>
62	    let meta: { env?: Record<string, string> } = {}
63	
64	    if (body.hook_payload) {
65	      hookPayload = body.hook_payload as Record<string, unknown>
66	      meta = (body.meta as typeof meta) || {}
67	    } else {
68	      hookPayload = body
69	    }
70	
71	    if (LOG_LEVEL === 'debug' || LOG_LEVEL === 'trace') {
72	      const logKeys = Object.keys(hookPayload).join(', ')
73	      const payload = JSON.stringify(hookPayload)
74	      const logPayload =
75	        LOG_LEVEL === 'trace'
76	          ? `Payload: ${payload}`
77	          : `Keys: ${logKeys} \nPayload: ${payload.slice(0, 500)}`
78	
79	      if (hookPayload.hook_event_name) {
80	        const toolInfo = hookPayload.tool_name ? `tool:${hookPayload.tool_name} tool_use_id:${hookPayload.tool_use_id}` : ''
81	        console.log(`[HOOK:${hookPayload.hook_event_name}] ${toolInfo} \n${logPayload}\n---`)
82	      } else {
83	        console.log('[EVENT]', logPayload)
84	      }
85	    }
86	
87	    const parsed = parseRawEvent(hookPayload)
88	
89	    // Resolve project - only on first event for this session
90	    const existingSession = await store.getSessionById(parsed.sessionId)
91	    let effectiveProjectId: number
92	
93	    if (existingSession) {
94	      effectiveProjectId = existingSession.project_id
95	    } else {
96	      const projectSlugOverride = meta.env?.AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG || null
97	      const resolved = await resolveProject(store, {
98	        sessionId: parsed.sessionId,
99	        slug: projectSlugOverride,
100	        transcriptPath: parsed.transcriptPath,
101	      })
102	      effectiveProjectId = resolved.projectId
103	    }
104	
105	    await store.upsertSession(
106	      parsed.sessionId,
107	      effectiveProjectId,
108	      parsed.slug,
109	      Object.keys(parsed.metadata).length > 0 ? parsed.metadata : null,
110	      parsed.timestamp,
111	    )
112	
113	    const rootAgentId = await ensureRootAgent(store, parsed.sessionId)
114	
115	    // When PreToolUse:Agent fires, stash name + description for early naming.
116	    // We store it both by toolUseId (for definitive lookup at PostToolUse) and
117	    // in a per-session FIFO queue (for early naming when subagent events arrive
118	    // before PostToolUse, since those events don't carry the parent tool_use_id).
119	    if (parsed.subtype === 'PreToolUse' && parsed.toolName === 'Agent') {
120	      const meta: PendingAgentMeta = { name: parsed.subAgentName, description: parsed.subAgentDescription }
121	      if (meta.name || meta.description) {
122	        if (parsed.toolUseId) {
123	          pendingAgentMeta.set(parsed.toolUseId, meta)
124	        }
125	        const queue = pendingAgentMetaQueue.get(parsed.sessionId) || []
126	        queue.push(meta)
127	        pendingAgentMetaQueue.set(parsed.sessionId, queue)
128	      }
129	      // Stash agent type from tool_input.subagent_type
130	      const agentType = (hookPayload as any)?.tool_input?.subagent_type
131	      if (agentType && parsed.toolUseId) {
132	        pendingAgentTypes.set(parsed.toolUseId, agentType)
133	      }
134	    }
135	
136	    // If the event has an ownerAgentId (from payload.agent_id), this event
137	    // belongs to that agent. Ensure the agent record exists.
138	    if (parsed.ownerAgentId && parsed.ownerAgentId !== rootAgentId) {
139	      // Only consume a queue entry for agents we haven't named yet.
140	      const sessionNamed = namedAgents.get(parsed.sessionId)
141	      const alreadyNamed = sessionNamed?.has(parsed.ownerAgentId) ?? false
142	      let pending: PendingAgentMeta | null = null
143	
144	      if (!alreadyNamed) {
145	        const queue = pendingAgentMetaQueue.get(parsed.sessionId)
146	        if (queue && queue.length > 0) {
147	          pending = queue.shift()!
148	          if (queue.length === 0) {
149	            pendingAgentMetaQueue.delete(parsed.sessionId)
150	          }
151	        }
152	        if (pending) {
153	          if (!sessionNamed) {
154	            namedAgents.set(parsed.sessionId, new Set([parsed.ownerAgentId]))
155	          } else {
156	            sessionNamed.add(parsed.ownerAgentId)
157	          }
158	        }
159	      }
160	
161	      // Extract agent_type from the hook payload — every subagent event carries it
162	      const ownerAgentType: string | null = (hookPayload as any)?.agent_type ?? null
163	
164	      await store.upsertAgent(
165	        parsed.ownerAgentId,
166	        parsed.sessionId,
167	        rootAgentId,
168	        pending?.name ?? null,
169	        pending?.description ?? null,
170	        ownerAgentType,
171	      )
172	    }
173	    let agentId = parsed.ownerAgentId || rootAgentId
174	
175	    // Create/update subagent records (from Agent tool PostToolUse or SubagentStop)
176	    if (parsed.subAgentId) {
177	      let subAgentName = parsed.subAgentName
178	      let subAgentDescription = parsed.subAgentDescription
179	      let subAgentType: string | null = (hookPayload as any)?.agent_type ?? null
180	      if (parsed.subtype === 'PostToolUse' && parsed.toolName === 'Agent' && parsed.toolUseId) {
181	        const metaFromPre = pendingAgentMeta.get(parsed.toolUseId)
182	        if (metaFromPre) {
183	          subAgentName = subAgentName || metaFromPre.name
184	          subAgentDescription = subAgentDescription || metaFromPre.description
185	          pendingAgentMeta.delete(parsed.toolUseId)
186	        }
187	        // Agent type: prefer stashed value from PreToolUse, then tool_input/tool_response
188	        const toolResponse = (hookPayload as any)?.tool_response
189	        subAgentType = pendingAgentTypes.get(parsed.toolUseId)
190	          ?? (hookPayload as any)?.tool_input?.subagent_type
191	          ?? toolResponse?.agentType
192	          ?? toolResponse?.subagent_type
193	          ?? subAgentType
194	        pendingAgentTypes.delete(parsed.toolUseId)
195	      }
196	
197	      await store.upsertAgent(
198	        parsed.subAgentId,
199	        parsed.sessionId,
200	        rootAgentId,
201	        subAgentName,
202	        subAgentDescription,
203	        subAgentType,
204	      )
205	
206	      // agent_progress events belong to the subagent
207	      if (parsed.subtype === 'agent_progress') {
208	        agentId = parsed.subAgentId
209	      }
210	    }
211	
212	    // Session lifecycle: SessionEnd stops the session, any other event reactivates a stopped session.
213	    if (parsed.subtype === 'SessionEnd') {
214	      await store.updateSessionStatus(parsed.sessionId, 'stopped')
215	      broadcastToAll({
216	        type: 'session_update',
217	        data: { id: parsed.sessionId, status: 'stopped' },
218	      })
219	    } else {
220	      const session = await store.getSessionById(parsed.sessionId)
221	      if (session && session.status === 'stopped') {
222	        await store.updateSessionStatus(parsed.sessionId, 'active')
223	        broadcastToAll({
224	          type: 'session_update',
225	          data: { id: parsed.sessionId, status: 'active' },
226	        })
227	      }
228	    }
229	
230	    // Set status for tool events
231	    let status = 'pending'
232	    if (parsed.subtype === 'PreToolUse') status = 'running'
233	    else if (parsed.subtype === 'PostToolUse') status = 'completed'
234	
235	    const eventId = await store.insertEvent({
236	      agentId,
237	      sessionId: parsed.sessionId,
238	      type: parsed.type,
239	      subtype: parsed.subtype,
240	      toolName: parsed.toolName,
241	      summary: null, // computed client-side
242	      timestamp: parsed.timestamp,
243	      payload: parsed.raw,
244	      toolUseId: parsed.toolUseId,
245	      status,
246	    })
247	
248	    const event: ParsedEvent = {
249	      id: eventId,
250	      agentId,
251	      sessionId: parsed.sessionId,
252	      type: parsed.type,
253	      subtype: parsed.subtype,
254	      toolName: parsed.toolName,
255	      toolUseId: parsed.toolUseId,
256	      status,
257	      timestamp: parsed.timestamp,
258	      payload: parsed.raw,
259	    }
260	
261	    broadcastToSession(parsed.sessionId, { type: 'event', data: event })
262	
263	    // Build response -- request local data if the server is missing info
264	    const requests: Array<{ cmd: string; args: Record<string, unknown>; callback: string }> = []
265	
266	    // Request session slug if missing
267	    if (parsed.raw.transcript_path) {
268	      const session = await store.getSessionById(parsed.sessionId)
269	      if (session && !session.slug) {
270	        requests.push({
271	          cmd: 'getSessionSlug',
272	          args: { transcript_path: parsed.raw.transcript_path },
273	          callback: `/api/sessions/${encodeURIComponent(parsed.sessionId)}/metadata`,
274	        })
275	      }
276	    }
277	
278	    const responseBody: Record<string, unknown> = {
279	      status: 'OK',
280	      meta: {
281	        event_id: eventId,
282	        session_id: parsed.sessionId,
283	        project_id: effectiveProjectId,
284	      },
285	    }
286	
287	    if (requests.length > 0) {
288	      responseBody.requests = requests
289	    }
290	
291	    return c.json(responseBody, 201)
292	  } catch (error) {
293	    console.error('Error processing event:', error)
294	    return c.json({ error: 'Invalid request' }, 400)
295	  }
296	})
297	
298	// GET /events/:id/thread
299	router.get('/events/:id/thread', async (c) => {
300	  const store = c.get('store')
301	  const eventId = parseInt(c.req.param('id'))
302	  const rows = await store.getThreadForEvent(eventId)
303	  const events: ParsedEvent[] = rows.map((r) => ({
304	    id: r.id,
305	    agentId: r.agent_id,
306	    sessionId: r.session_id,
307	    type: r.type,
308	    subtype: r.subtype,
309	    toolName: r.tool_name,
310	    toolUseId: r.tool_use_id || null,
311	    status: r.status || 'pending',
312	    timestamp: r.timestamp,
313	    payload: JSON.parse(r.payload),
314	  }))
315	  return c.json(events)
316	})
317	
318	/** Remove a single session from the in-memory root agent cache */
319	export function removeSessionRootAgent(sessionId: string): void {
320	  sessionRootAgents.delete(sessionId)
321	  pendingAgentMetaQueue.delete(sessionId)
322	  namedAgents.delete(sessionId)
323	}
324	
325	/** Clear all in-memory session state */
326	export function clearSessionRootAgents(): void {
327	  sessionRootAgents.clear()
328	  pendingAgentMeta.clear()
329	  pendingAgentMetaQueue.clear()
330	  namedAgents.clear()
331	}
332	
333	export default router

## Architecture Summary: Agents Observe

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how this project gathers, stores, and flows data:

---

### 1. **DATA GATHERING (How Data Is Collected)**

The system captures data through **Claude Code Hooks** - event listeners that fire on specific Claude Code agent lifecycle events.

**Source of Data:**
- Claude Code agent execution events via hooks
- 27 different hook event types are captured (defined in `/hooks/hooks.json`)

**Key Hook Events Captured:**
- Session lifecycle: `SessionStart`, `SessionEnd`
- User actions: `UserPromptSubmit`
- Tool execution: `PreToolUse`, `PostToolUse`, `PostToolUseFailure`
- Subagent lifecycle: `SubagentStart`, `SubagentStop`
- System events: `Stop`, `Notification`, `PermissionRequest`, `TaskCreated`, `TaskCompleted`, etc.
- Configuration changes: `ConfigChange`, `CwdChanged`, `FileChanged`
- Worktree operations: `WorktreeCreate`, `WorktreeRemove`

**Data Collection Flow:**
```
Claude Code Hook Event
  ↓ (event payload JSON passed via stdin)
hook.sh (simple bash wrapper)
  ↓ (pipes to Node.js CLI)
observe_cli.mjs (hook.sh command in hooks.json)
  ↓ (HTTP POST with event payload)
Server POST /api/events
```

**Data Format:**
- Raw event JSON is posted to the server with metadata envelope:
  ```javascript
  {
    hook_payload: { /* raw Claude Code event */ },
    meta: { env: { AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG: "..." } }
  }
  ```

---

### 2. **DATA STORAGE (Where Data Is Stored)**

All data is stored in a **SQLite database** located at `./data/observe.db` (configurable via `AGENTS_OBSERVE_DB_PATH`).

**Database Schema (4 tables):**

1. **`projects`** - Project metadata
   - Columns: `id`, `slug`, `name`, `transcript_path`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
   - Relates to one or more sessions
   - Project slug auto-detected from session transcript path or can be overridden via `AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG` env var

2. **`sessions`** - Claude Code session instances
   - Columns: `id`, `project_id`, `slug`, `status` (active/stopped), `started_at`, `stopped_at`, `metadata`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
   - One session = one Claude Code run
   - Session slug is a human-readable name (e.g., "twinkly-hugging-dragon") fetched from transcript file

3. **`agents`** - Agent instances within a session
   - Columns: `id`, `session_id`, `parent_agent_id`, `name`, `description`, `agent_type`, `agent_class`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
   - Root agent ID = session ID
   - Subagents have the root agent as parent
   - Agent metadata (name, description, type) extracted from Agent tool invocations

4. **`events`** - All captured events
   - Columns: `id`, `agent_id`, `session_id`, `type`, `subtype`, `tool_name`, `summary`, `timestamp`, `payload` (JSON), `tool_use_id`, `status`
   - Full raw payload stored as JSON string
   - Indexed heavily for query performance (session, agent, type, tool_use_id, timestamp)

**Storage Optimizations (SQLite PRAGMAs):**
- WAL mode (Write-Ahead Logging) for concurrent read/write
- 64MB cache (vs. default 2MB)
- Memory-mapped I/O (30MB)
- Indexes on commonly queried fields

---

### 3. **DATA FLOW (End-to-End Pipeline)**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 1. DATA COLLECTION                                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
Claude Code Agent Event
  → hook.sh (reads stdin, pipes to observe_cli.mjs)
  → observe_cli.mjs "hook" command (creates envelope with projectSlug)
  → HTTP POST to /api/events (fire-and-forget by default)

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 2. SERVER-SIDE PROCESSING (events.ts router)                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
POST /api/events received
  → Parse hook payload from envelope
  → Run parser.ts: extract structural fields
    • Event type/subtype (SessionStart, PreToolUse, etc.)
    • Tool name (if applicable)
    • Agent IDs (root + subagents)
    • Subagent metadata (name, description, type)
  → Resolve or create project
    • Check if session exists
    • If new: resolve project from transcript_path or AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG
  → Upsert session (create if new, update if exists)
  → Ensure root agent exists for this session
  → Handle subagent spawning/stopping:
    • Track pending Agent tool metadata by tool_use_id
    • Use FIFO queue for early naming (events may arrive before PostToolUse)
    • Update agent records with name/description/type
  → Insert event into database with all raw payload
  → Broadcast to subscribed WebSocket clients
    • Only sends to clients viewing this session
  → Return response with optional callback requests
    • May request session slug from local transcript file
    • Callback URL: /api/sessions/{sessionId}/metadata

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 3. WEBSOCKET BROADCAST (Real-Time Updates)                           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
Server maintains WebSocket connections on /api/events/stream
  → Client sends: { type: 'subscribe', sessionId: '...' }
  → Server broadcasts events only to subscribed session viewers
  → Each client only receives events for its viewed session

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 4. CLIENT-SIDE RENDERING                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
React Dashboard (app/client)
  → On mount: useWebSocket hook
    • Fetches initial events: GET /api/events/sessions/{sessionId}
    • Opens WebSocket to /api/events/stream
    • Subscribes to session
  → useUIStore (Zustand):
    • Maintains selectedSessionId
    • Caches events locally
  → Real-time updates:
    • New events appended to local cache via WebSocket
    • PreToolUse + PostToolUse merged into single row (client-side dedup)
    • All agent state (status, timing, event counts) derived from event stream
  → Rendering:
    • Sidebar: projects → sessions → agents hierarchy
    • Main panel: event stream with filtering/search
    • Summary text generated client-side from event payload
    • Emoji icons from config files

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 5. REST API QUERIES                                                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
GET /api/projects                    → List all projects
GET /api/projects/{id}/sessions      → Sessions in project
GET /api/sessions/{id}               → Session details
GET /api/sessions/{id}/agents        → Agents in session
GET /api/sessions/{id}/events        → Events with filters
  └─ ?agentIds=X,Y                  → Filter by agents
  └─ ?type=tool&search=...          → Filter + search
GET /api/events/{id}/thread          → Get thread context for event
```

---

### 4. **DEPLOYMENT MODES**

**Docker (Plugin/Production):**
- Container: `agents-observe` 
- Volumes: `./data:/data` (persists database)
- Port: 4981 (default, configurable)
- Client bundled into server (single port serves both API + dashboard)
- Environment variables injected via docker-compose

**Local Dev:**
- Separate Node processes: server on port 4981, client on port 5174
- Same SQLite database in `./data`
- Hot reload for both server and client

---

### 5. **KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS**

| Pattern | Implementation | Purpose |
|---------|---|---|
| **Dumb Pipe Hook Script** | observe_cli.mjs POST to API | Minimal logic in hook, all parsing on server |
| **Raw Payload Storage** | Full JSON stored in events table | Enables flexible client-side summarization |
| **Client-Driven State** | Events stream is source of truth | Server is stateless store |
| **Per-Session Broadcasting** | WebSocket clients subscribe to sessionId | Reduces broadcast overhead |
| **Subagent Metadata Queue** | FIFO queue for concurrent Agent tools | Handles race condition when events arrive before PostToolUse |
| **Tool Deduplication** | Client merges PreToolUse + PostToolUse | UI shows single "completed" tool row |
| **Lazy Session Naming** | Server requests slug from local transcript | Avoids storing full transcript path |

---

### 6. **CONFIGURATION**

**Environment Variables:**
- `AGENTS_OBSERVE_PROJECT_SLUG` - Override auto-detected project
- `AGENTS_OBSERVE_API_BASE_URL` - Hook POST target (default: `http://127.0.0.1:4981/api`)
- `AGENTS_OBSERVE_SERVER_PORT` - Server port (default: 4981)
- `AGENTS_OBSERVE_DB_PATH` - SQLite database location
- `AGENTS_OBSERVE_LOG_LEVEL` - debug/trace/info/warn
- `AGENTS_OBSERVE_RUNTIME` - docker/local

**Hook Configuration:**
- 27 event hooks registered in `hooks.json`
- Configured per-project in `.claude/settings.json` or globally in `~/.claude/settings.json`
- Template generated by: `just setup-hooks <project-name>`

This architecture prioritizes **observability without instrumentation overhead** - the hook script is a minimal pipe, all structural parsing happens server-side, and the React client derives state entirely from the immutable event stream, making the system resilient and auditable.
